Lead Full-Stack Engineer Marker Learning | Remote (U.S. only) | Full-time | Engineering | 185-250k

I have dyslexia. I was one of the lucky ones. Diagnosed at eight, I got accommodations and support before things spiraled.

Most kids aren't so lucky. The path from initial concern to diagnosis can take years and cost families upward of $5,000. For many, it never comes at all. In one study, 50% of prisoners were found to be dyslexic, with 80% functionally illiterate. Behind every missed diagnosis is a kid who gets told they're lazy, when the reality is no one ever gave them the right test.

Marker Learning is building an AI-enhanced assessment platform that makes identifying learning differences faster, cheaper, and more equitable, especially for multilingual students who are routinely misidentified or overlooked. Imagine if every kid could be screened for learning differences as easily as they're screened for vision. That's what we're building.

This isn't a chatbot or a homework helper. We're a Series B company backed by Reach Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, and Primary, running a multi-million-dollar longitudinal study spanning K–12 and developing the psychometric engine behind it. We need engineers who are excited by that.

We're hiring a hands-on full-stack engineer (React, Node.js, TypeScript) to help build and scale the platform: adaptive testing infrastructure, AI-powered scoring scoring and data pipelines, and the systems that maintain clinical rigor at scale. High autonomy, lightweight process, fast-paced. 8–10+ years, ideally with 0-to-1 or early-stage experience. You'll work directly with psychometrics, product, and data science, and the engineering decisions you make now will define the platform for years.

Reach out directly with your resume and why you want this position to lucie@markerlearning.com. Make the subject "Lead Engineer Application (I am not a bot)".

We’re a start-up company, we are building a local-AI camera OS that replaces cloud CCTV: SigmaStar cameras → RTSP/ONVIF → HDMI node → on-device AI (offline-first).

1. Hiring: OS / Device Lifecycle Engineer (Secure Boot / OTA / Identity) (Remote, UK/EU preferred)

We already run RTSP + inference. Now we need the person who owns several year device reality.

You will own (single layer ownership): Secure boot / trust chain (ROM → U-Boot → kernel → rootfs) OTA updates + rollback strategy (A/B, recovery modes) Device identity & lifecycle (provisioning, key rotation) Failure recovery (power loss, corrupted SD, watchdogs)

You must be able to answer directly: SD vs eMMC vs NVMe tradeoffs + corruption mitigation Brown-out behavior, thermal throttling, BSP maturity Camera ISP/NPU concurrency constraints + vendor SDK realities

2. Hiring: Independent Embedded/Firmware Engineer (Bring-up Audit & Stability) (Remote UK/EU preferred)

We’re shipping on real hardware. Reliability beats elegance. We need a senior embedded engineer to audit and validate our assumptions and prevent single-point-of-failure risk.

You will own: Independent review of firmware/bring-up assumptions Validation of performance, thermals, power behavior, stability Reproducible test plans (what breaks, how to detect, how to recover) Documentation that prevents knowledge living in one head

If you’ve shipped embedded systems that survive ugly reality (packet loss, unplugging, thermal throttling, storage corruption), we want to talk.

Competitive salary, flexible working environment & location, if you are interested, feel free to email: ayeeye.careers@gmail.com (subject: “HN – Role”)

Herakles Defense | Munich, Germany | REMOTE| Full-time | Part-time available

Here's the problem: A small drone flies at you at 60mph / 100 km/h You have a camera, one edge processor, and must detect, classify, and track it at 30fps — against sky, trees, buildings. Starting from 100+ meters. If your pipeline drops a single frame at far range, the intercept fails. Sounds impossible? We think together we can solve this.

Now the second problem: Your guidance law needs to predict where that target will be in 0.5 seconds, compute the intercept geometry, and command the flight controller — with noisy IMU data, wind gusts, and a target that doesn't fly in straight lines. No GPS. No RF link. Just onboard compute.

We solved the guidance part in simulation. Our IBVS pipeline tracks and hits moving targets in SITL. The hardware prototype is built and ready. But the real engineering starts now.

We need:

* Computer Vision & Edge AI Engineer — YOLO on Jetson/Hailo/QRB5165. Detect, classify and track small targets at 100m+. Model pruning, TensorRT, sensor fusion. If you've pushed edge hardware to its absolute limits, this is your problem.

* GNC / Flight Systems Engineer — Image base visual servoing, proportional navigation, EKF/UKF state estimation, PX4 integration. Take what works in sim and make it survive real-world sensor noise.

* Simulation & Test Engineer — SITL/HITL, Monte Carlo campaigns, digital twins. 10,000 simulated engagements before anything leaves the ground.

* Technical Advisors — Drone warfare, GNC/aerospace, defense procurement. A few hours/month from the right person changes our trajectory.

We're a pre-seed defense startup in Munich building autonomous interceptor drones to stop FPV kamikaze drones. 2 founders. Salary + equity. No AI screening — we read every application.

* a unique deployment model (self-host on-prem or in your cloud or run in our cloud)

* a mature product (the code base is nine+ years old and we've found and fixed a lot of the sharp edges around core login use cases; but don't worry, there are plenty more features to add)

* our CTO is the founder and still writes code

* a full featured free-as-in-beer version which makes the sales cycle easier; prospects often come in having prototyped an integration already
